mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as borrowing costs rise across the economy.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, which directly boosts corporate earnings forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60d forward returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction term Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above its long-term trend signals expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5d forward returns occur in < 50% of triggered instances during late-cycle expansions, or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels exceeding 250 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that increases household income and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 20d forward returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction term Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2 percent reflects sustained economic expansion that supports corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns during expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 100 while the federal funds rate remains elevated signals a supply-side contraction under tight monetary conditions, which reduces corporate output expectations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or interaction Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.
